text: Maximal lactate steady-state power output (PMLSS) corresponds to approximately 75% of VO2max in recreational male cyclists.
Blood lactate concentration at maximal lactate steady-state averages 5.3 mmol/l with high inter-individual variability (SD ±1.5 mmol/l).
Heart rate at maximal lactate steady-state averages 166 bpm (±10 bpm) in recreational male cyclists.
Power output at maximal lactate steady-state is highly reliable across days, with a coefficient of variation of only 3% and an intra-class correlation of 0.98.
Heart rate at maximal lactate steady-state has acceptable day-to-day reliability with a coefficient of variation of 6.3% and an intra-class correlation of 0.92.
Blood lactate concentration at MLSS has substantially higher day-to-day variability (CV 16.6%, ICC 0.71) than power or heart rate, making it a less reliable marker for prescribing training intensity.
Maximal lactate steady-state is defined as the highest workload maintainable without blood lactate accumulation exceeding 0.05 mmol/l/min during the last 20 minutes of a 30-minute constant-load test.
